sections.title: The Girdles (Connectors)
points: Pectoral Girdle (Shoulder): Consists of the Scapula and Clavicle. It has a shallow socket called the Glenoid cavity for high arm mobility.
Pelvic Girdle (Hip): Formed by the Ilium, Ischium, and Pubis fused together. It has a deep, stable socket called the Acetabulum.
Function: Girdles connect the limbs to the Axial Skeleton (the spine).

sections.title: Bones of the Arms vs. Legs
Digits: Hands and Feet
points: Metacarpals: 5 bones forming the framework of the palm.
Metatarsals: 5 bones forming the arch/sole of the foot.
Phalanges: 14 bones in each limb. Each finger/toe has 3 bones, except the Thumb and Big Toe which only have 2.

sections.text: The Radius is on the Thumb side! In the leg, the Tibia is the thick 'Shin' bone that carries your weight, while the Fibula is the thin outer bone used for muscle attachment.

sections.title: Why the Pelvis is Important
points: It supports the weight of the upper body when sitting or standing.
It protects delicate internal organs like the Bladder and Reproductive system (Uterus/Ovaries).
It provides a very strong attachment point for the powerful muscles of the legs.

cards.fact: The thigh bone; longest and strongest in the body.
Radius is lateral (thumb side); Ulna is medial (pinky side).
The sesamoid bone that protects the knee joint.
cards.mnemonic: The Patella is the Plate protecting your knee!
